finding.summary: The track has strong anti-phase stereo content, risking poor mono compatibility and a hollow or phasey sound on stereo systems.
finding.probable_cause: The pad sound design uses opposite polarity between left and right channels, likely from a wide stereo recording, a chorus/phaser effect, or a stereo widening process. The side signal is 12 dB louder than the mid, confirming that out-of-phase energy dominates.
finding.confidence_reason: Verified isolated track capture with low silence fraction (0.05). Stereo correlation is -0.42, side RMS is -15.0 dBFS while mid RMS is -27.0 dBFS, providing unambiguous evidence of strong anti-phase content.

evidence_refs.description: Stereo correlation of -0.42 indicates significant out-of-phase relationship.
Mid RMS level is very low (-27.0 dBFS), confirming lack of in-phase common energy.
Side RMS level is much louder (-15.0 dBFS) than mid, showing anti-phase energy dominates.
Low silence fraction (0.05) ensures the measured statistics are representative of the whole audio.
Isolation verified, so no other track contamination skews the stereo measurements.

proposal.reason: No single track-level parameter (volume, pan, phase invert) can correct a fundamentally out-of-phase stereo recording. The fix requires re-recording, re-processing, or adding a stereo field manipulation plugin (e.g., a mid/side imager) – none of which can be performed via the available operations.
